title: anthemis montana subsp. petraea
intro: Anthemis montana subsp. petraea, also known as mountain chamomile or white daisy, is a low-growing, perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Europe, with white or pale yellow daisy-like flowers and a sweet fragrance. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming effects. The health benefits of Anthemis montana subsp. petraea include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs in traditional remedies to enhance its medicinal properties.
